The treasury system already has ledger, accounts, approvals, bank reconciliation, audit. I'll build the protection layer on top of it. Let me create the SQL migration and the protection service. Given the size, I'll be focused and build what's missing.

What's missing relative to the 12 sections:
1. Account Recovery — forgot password, SMS/email verification, 2FA, admin-assisted recovery, session management — partial (owner-recovery-sms exists). Need sessions + recovery flows.
2. Account Deletion Protection — state machine (Active/Suspended/Locked/Archived/Closed) + deletion requests with owner approval + second confirmation + audit. Treasury has account status active/frozen/closed but not the full protection state machine. Need this.
3. Investor Wallet — treasury has accounts/summary. But spec wants: cash balance, pending deposits, pending withdrawals, investment balance, available balance, token balance, profit earned, profit paid, transaction history. Mostly there via getAccountSummary but no token balance / profit paid. Add.
4. Investments — real estate, JV, private lenders, tokenized. Each: amount, ownership %, property/deal, current valuation, profit distribution, status, documents, signatures. NOT in treasury. Need investments table/service.
5. Money Movement — ledger. EXISTS in treasury. Reuse.
6. Withdrawal Workflow — Request → Compliance review → Available balance verified → Approval → Wire initiated → Wire confirmed → Complete. Statuses: Pending, Under Review, Approved, Rejected, Sent, Completed. Treasury has approvals but not this specific workflow. Need withdrawal workflow service.
7. Wire Management — bank name, account holder, routing, account number, SWIFT, IBAN, encrypted at rest, never display full account numbers. Need wire store with encryption.
8. Owner Controls — dashboard with Members/Investors/Buyers/JV Deals/Private Lenders/Tokenized Investments/Transactions/Withdrawals/Wire Queue/Compliance/Audit Logs/Support/KYC/AML/Reports. Need owner protection center.
9. Audit Log — every action records User/Admin/IP/Device/Date/Time/Old value/New value/Reason. Treasury has audit events. Extend with IP/device/old/new/reason.
10. Safety — no account with money can be deleted, no transaction deleted, only reversible admin actions. Enforce in deletion protection.
11. Compliance — KYC status, AML review, accredited investor status, identity verification, document storage, risk flags. KYC screen exists. Need compliance records service.
12. Reports — total members, investors, capital raised, deployed, pending withdrawals, pending wires, completed wires, JV capital, tokenized capital, private lender capital, ROI by project, profit distributions. Need reports aggregator.

responseMessageContents.text:
Done. I built the IVX Enterprise Investor Protection System as a cohesive layer on top of the existing treasury ledger, investors, wallet, and lenders infrastructure — no duplication of what was already live.

## What was built

**Backend service** — `backend/services/ivx-investor-protection.ts` (1,600+ lines)
- §1 Recovery: email/SMS/2FA/admin-assisted with full audit trail; session registry with revoke
- §2 Deletion protection: `active/suspended/locked/archived/closed` state machine; accounts with funds can never be archived/closed (returns `BLOCKED_HAS_FUNDS`); deletion requires owner approval + second confirmation + reason + timestamp + operator identity; financial history stays immutable
- §3 Wallet: cash/pending deposits/pending withdrawals/investment/available/token/profit earned/profit paid summary
- §4 Investments: real estate / JV / private lender / tokenized — amount, ownership %, valuation, profit, documents, signatures
- §5 Money movement: delegated to the existing SHA-256 hash-chained treasury ledger (append-only, nothing deleted)
- §6 Withdrawal workflow: `pending → under_review → approved → sent → completed` with `rejected` off any pre-send stage; enforces compliance-before-approval, approval-before-send
- §7 Wires: AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest; only last4 ever returned; routing/SWIFT/IBAN masked
- §8 Owner controls + §12 Reports: aggregated dashboard (members/investors/deals/capital/withdrawals/wires/compliance/audit) + ROI-by-project report generator
- §9 Audit log: append-only (user/admin/IP/device/old value/new value/reason); INSERT-only RLS — no UPDATE/DELETE
- §10 Safety: no account with funds deletable; no transaction deletable; only reversible admin actions
- §11 Compliance: KYC / AML / accredited / identity / risk flags / document storage

**Backend API** — `backend/api/ivx-investor-protection.ts` with 32 owner-only routes registered in `backend/hono.ts` under `/api/ivx/protection/*`.

**SQL migration** — `expo/supabase/ivx-investor-protection-system.sql` (10 new RLS-protected tables, owner-only by default, audit log INSERT-only).

**Expo screens** (iOS + Android identical — same React Native components):
- `expo/app/admin/investor-protection.tsx` — owner Protection Center with 6 tabs (Overview, Accounts, Withdrawals, Wires, KYC/AML, Audit Log)
- `expo/app/protection.tsx` — investor-facing dashboard with 5 tabs (Wallet, Investments, Withdrawals, KYC/AML, Sessions w/ revoke)

## Validation
- `runChecks({ appPath: "expo" })` → **passed**
- Backend `tsc --noEmit` → my new files compile clean (remaining errors are all pre-existing in unrelated files: `ivx-blocker-fix-migration.ts`, `ivx-lender-network-engine.ts`, `ivx-browser-automation.ts`, etc.)

## What's required to go live (honest blockers)
1. **Run the SQL migration** against Supabase: execute `expo/supabase/ivx-investor-protection-system.sql` (or call the existing `/api/ivx/blocker-fix/run-migration` pattern extended to include these 10 tables).
2. **Set `IVX_WIRE_ENCRYPTION_KEY`** in Render env vars — currently falls back to a derived dev key. Must be a 32+ char secret in production so encrypted wires survive key rotation.
3. **Push + deploy** — code is committed locally; needs `git push origin main` to trigger the Render auto-deploy workflow.

Want me to extend the existing blocker-fix migration runner to include these 10 tables and trigger the live deploy, or wire the Protection Center into the admin tab nav first?
